hero.title: Neurology
hero.desc: Explore the latest medical advances and our advice for preserving your neurological health.

ui.expert_note_desc: "Early diagnosis using specialized exams like EEG or ENMG is often the key to successful management."

p1.title: Migraines: Causes and Modern Solutions
p1.excerpt: Discover how to identify types of migraines and current treatments to lasting relief for your pain.
p1.content: A migraine is not just a simple headache. It is a complex neurological condition that can be disabling in daily life. It is often characterized by throbbing pain, usually on one side of the head, accompanied by nausea and hypersensitivity to light. Causes can vary: stress, hormonal changes, or environmental factors. Today, preventive treatments and lifestyle rules can significantly reduce the frequency of attacks.

p2.title: Understanding EEG: The Key Brain Exam
p2.excerpt: Why does your doctor request an EEG? How does this painless and essential exam take place?
p2.content: Electroencephalography (EEG) is a fundamental exam in neurology. It records the electrical activity of neurons using electrodes placed on the scalp. This exam is crucial for diagnosing epilepsy, but also for exploring sleep disorders, encephalopathies, or loss of consciousness. Completely painless and non-invasive, it provides valuable information about brain function in real-time.

p3.title: Sleep and Neurological Health
p3.excerpt: The link between the quality of your sleep and the proper functioning of your brain is closer than we think.
p3.content: Good sleep is the guarantee of a healthy brain. While we sleep, our brain performs a real biological "cleaning," consolidating memory and eliminating toxins. Sleep disorders (insomnia, apnea, restless legs syndrome) can have repercussions on mood, concentration, and increase the risk of neurological pathologies in the long term.

p4.title: Epilepsy: Understanding Seizures
p4.excerpt: Living with epilepsy requires appropriate management and a deep understanding of the disease.
p4.content: Epilepsy is one of the most common neurological conditions. It is defined by a durable predisposition to generate epileptic seizures, the result of a sudden and excessive electrical discharge in the brain. Contrary to popular belief, there are many forms of epilepsy, and the majority of patients can lead a normal life thanks to well-measured drug treatment.

p5.title: Stroke: Warning Signs
p5.excerpt: Every minute counts. Learn to recognize symptoms to react at the first signs.
p5.content: Stroke (Cerebrovascular Accident) is an absolute emergency. Facial distortion, weakness in an arm or leg, or difficulty speaking should alert immediately. Rapid management in a neurovascular unit limits sequelae and improves prognosis. Prevention involves controlling hypertension, diabetes, and cholesterol.

p6.title: Parkinson's Disease
p6.excerpt: A practical guide on symptoms, management, and therapeutic innovations.
p6.content: Parkinson's disease affects motor skills but also mood and sleep. It is linked to the degeneration of neurons producing dopamine. While tremors are the best-known sign, slowness of movement and stiffness are also characteristic. Today, although we do not yet know how to cure it, many treatments allow effective control of symptoms for many years.

p7.title: Everything About ENMG
p7.excerpt: How does an ENMG take place? This exam is essential for diagnosing nerve and muscle pathologies.
p7.content: ENMG (Electroneuromyogram) allows studying the functioning of nerves and muscles. It helps diagnose common pathologies such as carpal tunnel syndrome, sciatica from a herniated disc, or diabetic neuropathies. The exam takes place in two stages: analysis of nerve conduction and examination of muscles using a fine electrode needle.

p8.title: Tension Headaches
p8.excerpt: Stress is often the cause. Discover exercises and tips to relieve these frequent headaches.
p8.content: Unlike migraines, tension headaches are often linked to stress or muscle tension in the neck. The pain is usually diffuse, like a tight band around the skull. Better stress management, improved posture at work, and relaxation exercises are often more effective than simple painkillers in the long term.

p9.title: Pediatric Neurology
p9.excerpt: Neuro-motor development and clinical signs to monitor in children and infants.
p9.content: The child's brain is growing and has its own neurological specificities. From early screening for developmental disorders to management of childhood epilepsy, pediatric neurology requires a gentle and adapted approach. Monitoring motor and language development milestones is essential for acting early if necessary.
